Overall:
The following processes are considered effective for the removal of tebuconazole: advanced oxidation using Fenton treatment, Fe2 H2O2 (greater than 99 percent removal) and advanced oxidation using a photosensitizer (ZnO) and an oxidant (Na2S2O8) under natural sunlight irradiation (greater than 99 percent removal). Ultraviolet irridiation (UV) can be effective in the presence of both a photocatalyst (TiO2) and an oxidizing agent (NH4)2S2O8 achieving 93 removal.
Lesser extent of removal was achieved by adsoprtion using a natural hemp substrate (48 percent removal). This treatment is not suitable for drinking water treatment but is applicable to the mitigation of tebuconazole in agricultural ditches. 
Research was identified evaluating the following treatment technologies for the removal of tebuconazole:  
Hydrogen Peroxide, Other Treatment and Ultraviolet Irradiation.
